verse_specific_anchors: the four-property quartet (indestructible / eternal / unborn / undecaying) — each named individually as a distinct property of the spec, mirroring 2.21's specific avināśin / nitya / aja / avyaya
the rhetorical 'how can he kill or be killed' doublet preserved as agency over predecessors' code AND agency over one's own deprecation — both denied
the Rails→Go scenario explicitly tests against the case where the realised one is mistaken for either killer (the new authors) or victim (the predecessor)
verdict_reason: I tested against three candidate verses: BG 2.20 (the soul is unborn/undying — overlaps but lacks the doublet of agency over both directions), BG 2.22 (clothes/body simile — adjacent but does not turn on the rhetorical 'how can he kill or be killed' question), BG 2.30 ('the dweller in the body of all is eternal' — overlaps but is conclusion-positioned and lacks the 'who knows' / 'how can he' rhetorical structure). None of these have the specific 'realised one cannot be agent of killing' move. The analog is anchored on 2.21-specific structure: four-property quartet + the rhetorical-question doublet. PASS — not transferable.
